concerns.text: FR-006 requires validating the proxy assumption (static logs vs. ablation utility) on a hold-out set. Task T014 implements this validation, but the data flow is broken: T008 (ablation) runs on the 'training set' only. Without running ablation on the hold-out set, there is no ground-truth utility label for the hold-out set to compare against static logs. T014 cannot satisfy FR-006 without an ablation task for the hold-out set.

The spec's Edge Cases section mandates a hard minimum token floor (appending 'Current Objective' if < 256 tokens). Task T016 handles the MAXIMUM budget (4096) and T005 handles NaN/Inf entropy. No task explicitly implements the logic to append the critical layer when the predicted token count falls below the 256-token minimum.

The spec's Edge Cases section states the system MUST flag the training set size as insufficient if n < 300 and recommend expanding the dataset, while defaulting to a heuristic. Task T008c implements the fallback to a heuristic (fixed k=2) but lacks the explicit task to 'flag' the size and 'recommend expanding the dataset' as a distinct researcher alert/warning mechanism required by the spec.

FR-001 requires extracting 'ground-truth memory layer utility' defined as the measured impact on win rate from an ablation study. Task T008 performs the ablation study. However, the spec implies this utility is needed for the classifier training (FR-002). The current task flow (T008 -> T008b -> T009) assumes the ablation is done on the training set. If the ablation is only on the training set, the 'ground truth' for the hold-out set (needed for FR-006) is missing. A task to run ablation on the hold-out set is missing.

Task T024a (divergence detection) is listed as a dependency for T025 (statistical testing). However, the task description for T024a is vague ('Analyze paired trajectories... to detect non-deterministic divergence') and does not specify the mechanism (e.g., comparing state hashes of re-simulated runs vs. original logs). While not strictly 'missing', the lack of a clear implementation path for this critical decision point (McNemar vs. Permutation) makes the task description insufficient to guarantee the spec's statistical rigor.
